# Tools used across projects but not part of a language runtime.

# --- Beads (via go install) ---

- name: Install beads issue tracker
  shell: |
    su -l {{ user }} -c 'GOPATH="$HOME/go" PATH="/usr/local/go/bin:$HOME/go/bin:$PATH" go install github.com/steveyegge/beads/cmd/bd@latest'
  args:
    creates: "/home/{{ user }}/go/bin/bd"

# --- Claude Code (native install with auto-update support) ---

- name: Install Claude Code
  shell: |
    curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | sh
  args:
    creates: /usr/local/bin/claude
  become: true
  become_user: "{{ user }}"

1â†’<#
     2â†’.SYNOPSIS
     3â†’    Create, destroy, or list WSL2 development VMs.
     4â†’
     5â†’.DESCRIPTION
     6â†’    One-file bootstrap for provisioned WSL2 dev environments.
     7â†’    Downloads Ubuntu rootfs, creates a WSL2 instance, and runs
     8â†’    Ansible to install all development tooling.
     9â†’
    10â†’    All configuration lives in a single JSON file at
    11â†’    %LOCALAPPDATA%\WSLDev\config.json. First run prompts to
    12â†’    create it. Per-instance settings (SSH port) are in the
    13â†’    Instances map.
    14â†’
    15â†’    Resilient to failures: if provisioning fails partway (bad
    16â†’    passphrase, network error, etc.), re-running the script
    17â†’    detects the existing instance and re-provisions it.
    18â†’
    19â†’.EXAMPLE
    20â†’    .\wsl-dev.ps1                              # Prompts for hostname, creates instance
    21â†’    .\wsl-dev.ps1 -Name myhost                 # Creates instance named "myhost"
    22â†’    .\wsl-dev.ps1 -Action destroy -Name myhost # Destroys named instance
    23â†’    .\wsl-dev.ps1 -Action list                 # Lists all WSL instances
    24â†’#>
    25â†’
    26â†’param(
    27â†’    [ValidateSet("create", "destroy", "list")]
    28â†’    [string]$Action = "create",
    29â†’
    30â†’    [string]$Name
    31â†’)
    32â†’
    33â†’$ErrorActionPreference = "Stop"
    34â†’$instanceRoot = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\WSLDev"
    35â†’$cacheDir     = "$instanceRoot\.cache"
    36â†’$configPath   = "$instanceRoot\config.json"
    37â†’$baseName     = "Ubuntu-24.04"  # Store distro used to create base tarball
    38â†’
    39â†’# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    40â†’# Config â€” single file, written incrementally as each field is collected
    41â†’# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    42â†’
    43â†’function Save-Config {
    44â†’    param([hashtable]$Config)
    45â†’    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path (Split-Path $configPath) -Force | Out-Null
    46â†’    $Config | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5 | Set-Content $configPath
    47â†’}
    48â†’
    49â†’function Prompt-Field {
    50â†’    param(
    51â†’        [hashtable]$Config,
    52â†’        [string]$Key,
    53â†’        [string]$Prompt,
    54â†’        [string]$Saved
    55â†’    )
    56â†’    if (-not [string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($Saved)) {
    57â†’        Write-Host "  $($Key): $Saved (saved)"
    58â†’        return $Saved
    59â†’    }
    60â†’    $value = Read-Host $Prompt
    61â†’    $Config[$Key] = $value
    62â†’    Save-Config $Config
    63â†’    return $value
    64â†’}
    65â†’
    66â†’function Get-DevConfig {
    67â†’    # Load existing config or start with an empty one
    68â†’    $cfg = [ordered]@{}
    69â†’    $resumed = $false
    70â†’    if (Test-Path $configPath) {
    71â†’        $loaded = Get-Content $configPath -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json
    72â†’        foreach ($prop in $loaded.PSObject.Properties) {
    73â†’            $cfg[$prop.Name] = $prop.Value
    74â†’        }
    75â†’        # Check if config is complete
    76â†’        $required = @("User", "GitName", "GitEmail", "IdentityKeyPath", "SetupRepo", "Repos", "Instances")
    77â†’        $missing = $required | Where-Object { -not $cfg.Contains($_) -or [string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($cfg[$_]) }
    78â†’        # Also treat IdentityKeyPath as missing if the files don't exist on disk
    79â†’        $idkPath = $cfg["IdentityKeyPath"]
    80â†’        if (-not [string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($idkPath) -and (-not (Test-Path $idkPath) -or -not (Test-Path "$idkPath.pub"))) {
    81â†’            Write-Host "  Saved GitHub key not found: $idkPath"
    82â†’            $cfg.Remove("IdentityKeyPath")
    83â†’            Save-Config $cfg
    84â†’            $missing = @("IdentityKeyPath")
    85â†’        }
    86â†’        if (-not $missing) {
    87â†’            Write-Host "Using config from $configPath"
    88â†’            Write-Host "  To start fresh, delete the file and re-run.`n"
    89â†’            return [PSCustomObject]$cfg
    90â†’        }
    91â†’        $resumed = $true
    92â†’        Write-Host "Resuming setup from $configPath (some fields already saved)."
    93â†’        Write-Host "  To start fresh, delete the file and re-run.`n"
    94â†’    } else {
    95â†’        Write-Host "First-time setup -- each answer is saved immediately.`n"
    96â†’    }
    97â†’
    98â†’    # Collect fields, skipping any that are already set
    99â†’    # Default Linux username to current Windows session username (lowercase)
   100â†’    if (-not $cfg.Contains("User") -or [string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($cfg["User"])) {
   101â†’        $cfg["User"] = $env:USERNAME.ToLower()
   102â†’        Save-Config $cfg
   103â†’    }
   104â†’    $cfg["User"] = Prompt-Field $cfg "User" "Linux username" $cfg["User"]
   105â†’
   106â†’    $cfg["GitName"] = Prompt-Field $cfg "GitName" "Git author name" $cfg["GitName"]
   107â†’
   108â†’    $cfg["GitEmail"] = Prompt-Field $cfg "GitEmail" "Git author email (for commits)" $cfg["GitEmail"]
   109â†’
   110â†’    $savedKey = $cfg["IdentityKeyPath"]
   111â†’    $savedKeyValid = (-not [string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($savedKey)) -and (Test-Path $savedKey) -and (Test-Path "$savedKey.pub")
   112â†’    if ($savedKeyValid) {
   113â†’        Write-Host "  IdentityKeyPath: $savedKey (saved)"
   114â†’        $idKey = $savedKey
   115â†’    } else {
   116â†’        if (-not [string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($savedKey)) {
   117â†’            Write-Host "  Saved key not found: $savedKey -- re-selecting..."
   118â†’        }
   119â†’        # Discover SSH private keys (files with a matching .pub, excluding VM access keys)
   120â†’        $sshDir = "$env:USERPROFILE\.ssh"
   121â†’        $candidates = @()
   122â†’        if (Test-Path $sshDir) {
   123â†’            $candidates = @(Get-ChildItem "$sshDir\*.pub" -File |
   124â†’                Where-Object { $_.Name -notmatch '_wsl_' } |
   125â†’                ForEach-Object { $_.FullName -replace '\.pub$', '' } |
   126â†’                Where-Object { Test-Path $_ })
   127â†’        }
   128â†’        Write-Host ""
   129â†’        Write-Host "GitHub SSH key (used for git/GitHub auth, NOT for VM access):"
   130â†’        if ($candidates.Count -gt 0) {
   131â†’            for ($i = 0; $i -lt $candidates.Count; $i++) {
   132â†’                Write-Host "  [$($i + 1)] $($candidates[$i])"
   133â†’            }
   134â†’            Write-Host "  [M] Enter path manually"
   135â†’            $choice = Read-Host "Select a key"
   136â†’            if ($choice -match '^\d+$' -and [int]$choice -ge 1 -and [int]$choice -le $candidates.Count) {
   137â†’                $idKey = $candidates[[int]$choice - 1]
   138â†’            } elseif ($choice -match '^[Mm]$') {
   139â†’                $idKey = Read-Host "Path to SSH private key"
   140â†’            } else {
   141â†’                Write-Error "Invalid selection."
   142â†’                exit 1
   143â†’            }
   144â†’        } else {
   145â†’            Write-Host "  No SSH keys found in $sshDir"
   146â†’            $idKey = Read-Host "Path to SSH private key"
   147â†’        }
   148â†’        if (-not (Test-Path $idKey)) {
   149â†’            Write-Error "Key not found at $idKey"
   150â†’            exit 1
   151â†’        }
   152â†’        if (-not (Test-Path "$idKey.pub")) {
   153â†’            Write-Error "Public key not found at $idKey.pub"
   154â†’            exit 1
   155â†’        }
   156â†’        $cfg["IdentityKeyPath"] = $idKey
   157â†’        Save-Config $cfg
   158â†’    }
   159â†’
   160â†’    $cfg["SetupRepo"] = Prompt-Field $cfg "SetupRepo" "dev-setup repo SSH URL (e.g., git@github.com:org/dev-setup.git)" $cfg["SetupRepo"]
   161â†’
   162â†’    if (-not $cfg.Contains("Dotfiles")) {
   163â†’        $dotfiles = Read-Host "Dotfiles repo SSH URL (empty to skip)"
   164â†’        $cfg["Dotfiles"] = $dotfiles
   165â†’        Save-Config $cfg
   166â†’    } else {
   167â†’        $df = $cfg["Dotfiles"]
   168â†’        if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($df)) {
   169â†’            Write-Host "  Dotfiles: (none)"
   170â†’        } else {
   171â†’            Write-Host "  Dotfiles: $df (saved)"
   172â†’        }
   173â†’    }
   174â†’
   175â†’    if (-not $cfg.Contains("Repos") -or $cfg["Repos"] -eq $null) {
   176â†’        $repos = @()
   177â†’        Write-Host "`nProject repos to clone (SSH URLs, e.g., git@github.com:org/repo.git; empty line when done):"
   178â†’        while ($true) {
   179â†’            $repo = Read-Host "  repo"
   180â†’            if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($repo)) { break }
   181â†’            $repos += $repo
   182â†’        }
   183â†’        $cfg["Repos"] = $repos
   184â†’        Save-Config $cfg
   185â†’    } else {
   186â†’        $repoList = ($cfg["Repos"] | ForEach-Object { $_ }) -join ", "
   187â†’        Write-Host "  Repos: $repoList (saved)"
   188â†’    }
   189â†’
   190â†’    if (-not $cfg.Contains("Instances") -or $cfg["Instances"] -eq $null) {
   191â†’        $cfg["Instances"] = [ordered]@{}
   192â†’        Save-Config $cfg
   193â†’    }
   194â†’
   195â†’    Write-Host "`nConfig complete.`n"
   196â†’    return [PSCustomObject]$cfg
   197â†’}
   198â†’
   199â†’function Get-InstanceConfig {
   200â†’    param([PSCustomObject]$Config, [string]$InstanceName)
   201â†’
   202â†’    # Look up per-instance settings; fall back to defaults
   203â†’    $inst = $Config.Instances.PSObject.Properties | Where-Object { $_.Name -eq $InstanceName }
   204â†’    if ($inst) {
   205â†’        return $inst.Value
   206â†’    }
   207â†’    # Default for unlisted instances: auto-assign port based on instance count
   208â†’    $basePort = 2222
   209â†’    $existing = @($Config.Instances.PSObject.Properties).Count
   210â†’    return [PSCustomObject]@{ SSHPort = $basePort + $existing }
   211â†’}
   212â†’
   213â†’function Write-Utf8NoBom {
   214â†’    param([string]$Path, [string]$Content)
   215â†’    # Ensure Unix line endings (LF only) â€” bash chokes on \r\n
   216â†’    $Content = $Content -replace "`r`n", "`n"
   217â†’    [IO.File]::WriteAllText($Path, $Content, [Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false))
   218â†’}
   219â†’
   220â†’# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   221â†’# Check if a WSL2 instance exists (registered)
   222â†’# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   223â†’
   224â†’function Test-WslInstance {
   225â†’    param([string]$InstanceName)
   226â†’    # wsl --list output contains null characters; strip them before matching
   227â†’    $raw = (wsl --list --quiet 2>$null) -join "`n"
   228â†’    $clean = $raw -replace "`0", ""
   229â†’    return ($clean -split "`n" | ForEach-Object { $_.Trim() } | Where-Object { $_ -eq $InstanceName }).Count -gt 0
   230â†’}
   231â†’
   232â†’# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   233â†’# Create
   234â†’# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   235â†’
   236â†’function Invoke-Create {
   237â†’    $config = Get-DevConfig
   238â†’    $user = $config.User
   239â†’    $setupRepo = $config.SetupRepo
   240â†’
   241â†’    # Use -Name as the WSL instance name and hostname
   242â†’    if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($Name)) {
   243â†’        # Reuse the existing instance name if there's exactly one
   244â†’        $instances = @($config.Instances.PSObject.Properties)
   245â†’        if ($instances.Count -eq 1) {
   246â†’            $Name = $instances[0].Name
   247â†’            Write-Host "  Using instance: $Name (from config)"
   248â†’        } else {
   249â†’            $Name = Read-Host "Hostname for this instance (used as WSL name and Linux hostname)"
   250â†’            if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($Name)) {
   251â†’                Write-Error "Hostname is required."
   252â†’                exit 1
   253â†’            }
   254â†’        }
   255â†’    }
   256â†’
   257â†’    $inst = Get-InstanceConfig -Config $config -InstanceName $Name
   258â†’    $sshPort = $inst.SSHPort
   259â†’
   260â†’    # Save instance config if new
   261â†’    $existingInst = $config.Instances.PSObject.Properties | Where-Object { $_.Name -eq $Name }
   262â†’    if (-not $existingInst) {
   263â†’        $config.Instances | Add-Member -NotePropertyName $Name -NotePropertyValue ([PSCustomObject]@{ SSHPort = $sshPort }) -Force
   264â†’        $cfg = [ordered]@{}
   265â†’        foreach ($prop in $config.PSObject.Properties) { $cfg[$prop.Name] = $prop.Value }
   266â†’        Save-Config $cfg
   267â†’    }
   268â†’
   269â†’    $hostname = $Name
   270â†’
   271â†’    # --- Verify Windows SSH agent has the GitHub key loaded ---
   272â†’    $agentKeys = ssh-add -L 2>&1
   273â†’    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0 -or $agentKeys -match "Could not open|error") {
   274â†’        Write-Error "Windows SSH agent is not running. Start it with: Get-Service ssh-agent | Set-Service -StartupType Automatic; Start-Service ssh-agent"
   275â†’        exit 1
   276â†’    }
   277â†’    if ($agentKeys -match "no identities") {
   278â†’        Write-Error "No keys in Windows SSH agent. Add your GitHub key with: ssh-add $($config.IdentityKeyPath)"
   279â†’        exit 1
   280â†’    }
   281â†’    Write-Host "  Windows SSH agent has keys loaded."
   282â†’
   283â†’    # --- Generate VM access key (separate from identity key) ---
   284â†’    $vmKeyPath = "$env:USERPROFILE\.ssh\id_ed25519_wsl_$Name"
   285â†’    if (-not (Test-Path $vmKeyPath)) {
   286â†’        Write-Host "Generating VM access key at $vmKeyPath ..."
   287â†’        ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f $vmKeyPath -N '""' -C "wsl-$Name-access"
   288â†’        if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { throw "ssh-keygen failed" }
   289â†’    } else {
   290â†’        Write-Host "  VM access key exists: $vmKeyPath"
   291â†’    }
   292â†’
   293â†’    # --- Step 1: Ensure WSL2 instance exists ---
   294â†’    if (Test-WslInstance $Name) {
   295â†’        Write-Host "Instance '$Name' already exists. Re-provisioning..."
   296â†’    } else {
   297â†’        # Get base tarball from the official Store image (cached after first run)
   298â†’        New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $cacheDir -Force | Out-Null
   299â†’        $tarball = "$cacheDir\ubuntu-noble.tar.gz"
   300â†’        if (-not (Test-Path $tarball)) {
   301â†’            Write-Host "Creating base image from official Ubuntu Store distro..."
   302â†’
   303â†’            $installed = wsl --list --quiet 2>$null | Where-Object { $_ -match "^$baseName$" }
   304â†’            if (-not $installed) {
   305â†’                Write-Host "  Installing $baseName from Microsoft Store..."
   306â†’                wsl --install $baseName --no-launch
   307â†’                if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { throw "wsl --install $baseName failed" }
   308â†’            }
   309â†’
   310â†’            Write-Host "  Exporting to tarball (this takes a minute)..."
   311â†’            wsl --export $baseName $tarball
   312â†’            if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { throw "wsl --export failed" }
   313â†’
   314â†’            Write-Host "  Removing temporary Store distro..."
   315â†’            wsl --unregister $baseName
   316â†’            Write-Host "  Base image cached at $tarball"
   317â†’        } else {
   318â†’            Write-Host "Using cached base image."
   319â†’        }
   320â†’
   321â†’        $instancePath = "$instanceRoot\$Name"
   322â†’        Write-Host "`nCreating WSL2 instance '$Name'..."
   323â†’        New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $instancePath -Force | Out-Null
   324â†’        wsl --import $Name $instancePath $tarball --version 2
   325â†’        if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { throw "wsl --import failed" }
   326â†’
   327â†’        # Enable systemd
   328â†’        Write-Host "Enabling systemd..."
   329â†’        wsl -d $Name --exec bash -c "printf '[boot]\nsystemd=true\n' > /etc/wsl.conf"
   330â†’
   331â†’        # Restart to activate systemd
   332â†’        Write-Host "Restarting instance for systemd..."
   333â†’        wsl --terminate $Name
   334â†’        Start-Sleep -Seconds 3
   335â†’    }
   336â†’
   337â†’    # Ensure user exists (idempotent â€” runs on both new and re-provision)
   338â†’    Write-Host "Ensuring user '$user' exists..."
   339â†’    wsl -d $Name -u root --exec bash -c "set -euo pipefail; id $user &>/dev/null || useradd -m -s /bin/bash $user; usermod -aG sudo $user; echo '$user ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL' > /etc/sudoers.d/$user; chmod 440 /etc/sudoers.d/$user; grep -q '^\[user\]' /etc/wsl.conf || printf '\n[user]\ndefault=$user\n' >> /etc/wsl.conf"
   340â†’
   341â†’    # --- Step 2: Stage files ---
   342â†’    $stageDir = "$instanceRoot\.stage"
   343â†’    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $stageDir -Force | Out-Null
   344â†’
   345â†’    Copy-Item $vmKeyPath "$stageDir\vm_access_key"
   346â†’    Copy-Item "$vmKeyPath.pub" "$stageDir\vm_access_key.pub"
   347â†’    Copy-Item "$($config.IdentityKeyPath).pub" "$stageDir\id_ed25519.pub"
   348â†’
   349â†’    $dotfilesRepo = if ($config.Dotfiles) { $config.Dotfiles } else { "" }
   350â†’
   351â†’    # Get IANA timezone from the WSL instance (inherits from Windows)
   352â†’    $ianaTz = (wsl -d $Name -u root --exec cat /etc/timezone 2>$null)
   353â†’    if (-not $ianaTz) { $ianaTz = "Etc/UTC" }
   354â†’
   355â†’    $extraVars = [ordered]@{
   356â†’        user      = $user
   357â†’        git_name  = $config.GitName
   358â†’        git_email = $config.GitEmail
   359â†’        ssh_port  = $sshPort
   360â†’        hostname  = $hostname
   361â†’        timezone  = $ianaTz
   362â†’        dotfiles  = $dotfilesRepo
   363â†’        repos     = @($config.Repos | ForEach-Object { $_ })
   364â†’    }
   365â†’    Write-Utf8NoBom "$stageDir\extra-vars.json" ($extraVars | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5)
   366â†’
   367â†’    # Two provision scripts:
   368â†’    #   1. provision-root.sh â€” runs as root: installs SSH keys, apt packages, Ansible
   369â†’    #   2. provision-user.sh â€” runs as the user: git clones, Ansible (uses Windows SSH agent via ssh.exe)
   370â†’
   371â†’    $rootScript = @"
   372â†’#!/bin/bash
   373â†’set -euo pipefail
   374â†’
   375â†’USER_NAME="$user"
   376â†’HOME_DIR="/home/`$USER_NAME"
   377â†’
   378â†’# --- SSH keys ---
   379â†’echo '=== Installing SSH keys ==='
   380â†’mkdir -p `$HOME_DIR/.ssh
   381â†’chmod 700 `$HOME_DIR/.ssh
   382â†’
   383â†’# VM access key (from Windows) â†’ authorized_keys
   384â†’cp /tmp/stage/vm_access_key.pub `$HOME_DIR/.ssh/authorized_keys
   385â†’
   386â†’# Identity public key only (for GitHub reference; private key stays on Windows)
   387â†’cp /tmp/stage/id_ed25519.pub `$HOME_DIR/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub
   388â†’
   389â†’chown -R `$USER_NAME:`$USER_NAME `$HOME_DIR/.ssh
   390â†’chmod 644 `$HOME_DIR/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub `$HOME_DIR/.ssh/authorized_keys
   391â†’
   392â†’ssh-keyscan github.com >> `$HOME_DIR/.ssh/known_hosts 2>/dev/null
   393â†’chown `$USER_NAME:`$USER_NAME `$HOME_DIR/.ssh/known_hosts
   394â†’
   395â†’# --- System packages ---
   396â†’echo '=== Installing git and Ansible ==='
   397â†’apt-get update -qq
   398â†’apt-get install -y -qq git software-properties-common > /dev/null
   399â†’add-apt-repository --yes --update ppa:ansible/ansible > /dev/null 2>&1
   400â†’apt-get install -y -qq ansible > /dev/null
   401â†’
   402â†’echo '=== Root setup complete ==='
   403â†’"@
   404â†’    Write-Utf8NoBom "$stageDir\provision-root.sh" $rootScript
   405â†’
   406â†’    $userScript = @"
   407â†’#!/bin/bash
   408â†’set -euo pipefail
   409â†’
   410â†’SETUP_REPO="$setupRepo"
   411â†’
   412â†’# --- Use Windows SSH agent via ssh.exe (no private key copy needed) ---
   413â†’echo '=== Configuring SSH for provisioning ==='
   414â†’WIN_SSH="/mnt/c/Windows/System32/OpenSSH/ssh.exe"
   415â†’if [ ! -x "`$WIN_SSH" ]; then
   416â†’    echo "ERROR: Windows SSH not found at `$WIN_SSH"
   417â†’    exit 1
   418â†’fi
   419â†’export GIT_SSH_COMMAND="`$WIN_SSH"
   420â†’echo "  Using Windows SSH agent via `$WIN_SSH"
   421â†’
   422â†’# --- Clone dev-setup repo ---
   423â†’echo ''
   424â†’echo '=== Cloning dev-setup repo ==='
   425â†’if [ -d ~/dev-setup ]; then
   426â†’    echo '  Repo exists, pulling latest...'
   427â†’    cd ~/dev-setup && git pull --ff-only
   428â†’else
   429â†’    echo "  Cloning `$SETUP_REPO ..."
   430â†’    git clone --depth 1 "`$SETUP_REPO" ~/dev-setup
   431â†’fi
   432â†’
   433â†’# --- Run Ansible (installs all packages) ---
   434â†’echo ''
   435â†’echo '=== Running Ansible playbook ==='
   436â†’cd ~/dev-setup
   437â†’sudo --preserve-env=GIT_SSH_COMMAND ansible-playbook playbook.yml --diff -e @/tmp/stage/extra-vars.json
   438â†’
   439â†’# --- Dotfiles (after packages so tools like stow are available) ---
   440â†’DOTFILES_REPO="$dotfilesRepo"
   441â†’if [ -n "`$DOTFILES_REPO" ]; then
   442â†’    echo ''
   443â†’    echo '=== Installing dotfiles ==='
   444â†’    if [ -d ~/dotfiles ]; then
   445â†’        echo '  Dotfiles repo exists, pulling latest...'
   446â†’        cd ~/dotfiles && git pull --ff-only
   447â†’    else
   448â†’        echo "  Cloning `$DOTFILES_REPO ..."
   449â†’        git clone "`$DOTFILES_REPO" ~/dotfiles
   450â†’    fi
   451â†’    if [ -x ~/dotfiles/install.sh ]; then
   452â†’        echo '  Running install.sh...'
   453â†’        cd ~/dotfiles && ./install.sh
   454â†’    fi
   455â†’fi
   456â†’
   457â†’# --- Cleanup ---
   458â†’sudo rm -rf /tmp/stage
   459â†’
   460â†’echo ''
   461â†’echo '========================================='
   462â†’echo '  Provisioning complete.'
   463â†’echo '========================================='
   464â†’echo ''
   465â†’echo 'Remaining manual steps:'
   466â†’echo '  1. netbird up         (authenticate to mesh VPN)'
   467â†’echo ''
   468â†’"@
   469â†’    Write-Utf8NoBom "$stageDir\provision-user.sh" $userScript
   470â†’
   471â†’    # --- Step 3: Copy staging files in and run provisioning ---
   472â†’    Write-Host "`nProvisioning (this takes a few minutes)..."
   473â†’
   474â†’    $wslStageDir = wsl -d $Name --exec wslpath -a "$stageDir"
   475â†’
   476â†’    # Copy stage files in (as root)
   477â†’    wsl -d $Name -u root --exec bash -c "cp -r $wslStageDir /tmp/stage && chmod +x /tmp/stage/*.sh"
   478â†’
   479â†’    # Root phase: SSH keys + system packages (explicitly as root)
   480â†’    Write-Host ""
   481â†’    wsl -d $Name -u root --exec /tmp/stage/provision-root.sh
   482â†’    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) {
   483â†’        Write-Host "`nRoot provisioning failed. Re-run this script to retry."
   484â†’        Remove-Item -Recurse -Force $stageDir -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
   485â†’        exit 1
   486â†’    }
   487â†’
   488â†’    # User phase: ssh-agent, git clones, Ansible (explicitly as the user)
   489â†’    Write-Host ""
   490â†’    wsl -d $Name -u $user --exec /tmp/stage/provision-user.sh
   491â†’    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) {
   492â†’        Write-Host "`nUser provisioning failed. Re-run this script to retry."
   493â†’        Remove-Item -Recurse -Force $stageDir -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
   494â†’        exit 1
   495â†’    }
   496â†’
   497â†’    # Clean up staging dir on Windows side
   498â†’    Remove-Item -Recurse -Force $stageDir -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
   499â†’
   500â†’    # --- Step 4: Port forwarding + hostname alias (non-fatal, elevates for netsh/hosts) ---
   501â†’    Write-Host "`nSetting up SSH port forwarding (port $sshPort) and hostname alias..."
   502â†’    try {
   503â†’        $hostsCmd = "`$h = '$env:SystemRoot\System32\drivers\etc\hosts'; " +
   504â†’                    "`$entry = '127.0.0.1 $hostname'; " +
   505â†’                    "(Get-Content `$h) -notmatch '^\s*[\d\.]+\s+$hostname\s*$' | Set-Content `$h; " +
   506â†’                    "Add-Content `$h `$entry"
   507â†’        $netshCmd = "netsh interface portproxy delete v4tov4 listenport=$sshPort listenaddress=0.0.0.0 2>`$null; " +
   508â†’                    "netsh interface portproxy add v4tov4 listenport=$sshPort listenaddress=0.0.0.0 connectport=$sshPort connectaddress=localhost; " +
   509â†’                    $hostsCmd
   510â†’        Start-Process powershell -Verb RunAs -ArgumentList "-Command", $netshCmd -Wait -WindowStyle Hidden
   511â†’        # Verify the rule was actually created
   512â†’        $proxy = netsh interface portproxy show v4tov4 2>$null
   513â†’        if ($proxy -match "$sshPort") {
   514â†’            Write-Host "  Port forwarding configured."
   515â†’        } else {
   516â†’            Write-Host "  WARNING: Port forwarding rule not found. The elevated command may have failed."
   517â†’            Write-Host "  You can still access the instance via: wsl -d $Name"
   518â†’        }
   519â†’    } catch [System.InvalidOperationException] {
   520â†’        Write-Host "  WARNING: UAC prompt was declined. Port forwarding not configured."
   521â†’        Write-Host "  You can still access the instance via: wsl -d $Name"
   522â†’    } catch {
   523â†’        Write-Host "  WARNING: Port forwarding failed: $_"
   524â†’        Write-Host "  You can still access the instance via: wsl -d $Name"
   525â†’    }
   526â†’
   527â†’    # --- Step 5: Auto-start WSL instance at logon ---
   528â†’    $taskName = "WSL-AutoStart-$Name"
   529â†’    $existingTask = Get-ScheduledTask -TaskName $taskName -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
   530â†’    if ($existingTask) {
   531â†’        Write-Host "  Scheduled task '$taskName' already exists."
   532â†’    } else {
   533â†’        Write-Host "Registering scheduled task to auto-start '$Name' at logon..."
   534â†’        try {
   535â†’            $action = New-ScheduledTaskAction -Execute "wsl.exe" -Argument "-d $Name -- sleep infinity"
   536â†’            $trigger = New-ScheduledTaskTrigger -AtLogOn
   537â†’            $trigger.UserId = [System.Security.Principal.WindowsIdentity]::GetCurrent().Name
   538â†’            $settings = New-ScheduledTaskSettingsSet -AllowStartIfOnBatteries -DontStopIfGoingOnBatteries -ExecutionTimeLimit ([TimeSpan]::Zero)
   539â†’            Register-ScheduledTask -TaskName $taskName -Action $action -Trigger $trigger -Settings $settings | Out-Null
   540â†’            Write-Host "  Scheduled task '$taskName' registered."
   541â†’        } catch {
   542â†’            Write-Host "  WARNING: Could not register scheduled task: $_"
   543â†’            Write-Host "  WSL instance won't auto-start. You can start it manually with: wsl -d $Name"
   544â†’        }
   545â†’    }
   546â†’
   547â†’    # --- Step 6: Windows SSH config + agent ---
   548â†’    $sshConfigPath = "$env:USERPROFILE\.ssh\config"
   549â†’    $hostBlock = @"
   550â†’
   551â†’Host $hostname
   552â†’    HostName 127.0.0.1
   553â†’    Port $sshPort
   554â†’    User $user
   555â†’    IdentityFile $vmKeyPath
   556â†’"@
   557â†’    # Remove any existing block for this host, then append
   558â†’    if (Test-Path $sshConfigPath) {
   559â†’        $content = Get-Content $sshConfigPath -Raw
   560â†’        $content = $content -replace "(?m)\r?\nHost $hostname\r?\n(\s+\S[^\n]*\r?\n)*", ""
   561â†’        Set-Content $sshConfigPath $content.TrimEnd()
   562â†’    }
   563â†’    Add-Content $sshConfigPath $hostBlock
   564â†’    Write-Host "  SSH config updated: Host $hostname -> localhost:$sshPort"
   565â†’
   566â†’    # Add VM access key to Windows SSH agent
   567â†’    ssh-add $vmKeyPath 2>$null
   568â†’    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) {
   569â†’        Write-Host "  VM access key added to Windows SSH agent."
   570â†’    } else {
   571â†’        Write-Host "  WARNING: Could not add VM key to SSH agent. Run: ssh-add $vmKeyPath"
   572â†’    }
   573â†’
   574â†’    Write-Host ""
   575â†’    Write-Host "Instance '$Name' is ready."
   576â†’    Write-Host "  Enter:  wsl -d $Name"
   577â†’    Write-Host "  SSH:    ssh $hostname"
   578â†’    Write-Host ""
   579â†’}
   580â†’
   581â†’# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   582â†’# Destroy
   583â†’# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   584â†’
   585â†’function Invoke-Destroy {
   586â†’    if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($Name)) {
   587â†’        Write-Error "Destroy requires -Name <instance>."
   588â†’        exit 1
   589â†’    }
   590â†’    $config = Get-DevConfig
   591â†’    $inst = Get-InstanceConfig -Config $config -InstanceName $Name
   592â†’    $sshPort = $inst.SSHPort
   593â†’
   594â†’    Write-Host "Destroying instance '$Name'..."
   595â†’    # Remove auto-start scheduled task
   596â†’    $taskName = "WSL-AutoStart-$Name"
   597â†’    $existingTask = Get-ScheduledTask -TaskName $taskName -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
   598â†’    if ($existingTask) {
   599â†’        Unregister-ScheduledTask -TaskName $taskName -Confirm:$false
   600â†’        Write-Host "  Removed scheduled task '$taskName'"
   601â†’    }
   602â†’    wsl --unregister $Name 2>$null
   603â†’    $instancePath = "$instanceRoot\$Name"
   604â†’    if (Test-Path $instancePath) {
   605â†’        Remove-Item -Recurse -Force $instancePath
   606â†’    }
   607â†’    # Remove VM access key pair
   608â†’    $vmKeyPath = "$env:USERPROFILE\.ssh\id_ed25519_wsl_$Name"
   609â†’    if (Test-Path $vmKeyPath) {
   610â†’        Remove-Item -Force $vmKeyPath
   611â†’        Write-Host "  Removed $vmKeyPath"
   612â†’    }
   613â†’    if (Test-Path "$vmKeyPath.pub") {
   614â†’        Remove-Item -Force "$vmKeyPath.pub"
   615â†’        Write-Host "  Removed $vmKeyPath.pub"
   616â†’    }
   617â†’    # Remove SSH config block
   618â†’    $sshConfigPath = "$env:USERPROFILE\.ssh\config"
   619â†’    if (Test-Path $sshConfigPath) {
   620â†’        $content = Get-Content $sshConfigPath -Raw
   621â†’        $content = $content -replace "(?m)\r?\nHost $Name\r?\n(\s+\S[^\n]*\r?\n)*", ""
   622â†’        Set-Content $sshConfigPath $content.TrimEnd()
   623â†’        Write-Host "  Removed SSH config for Host $Name"
   624â†’    }
   625â†’    # Remove key from Windows SSH agent
   626â†’    ssh-add -d $vmKeyPath 2>$null
   627â†’    try {
   628â†’        $hostsCmd = "`$h = '$env:SystemRoot\System32\drivers\etc\hosts'; " +
   629â†’                    "(Get-Content `$h) -notmatch '^\s*[\d\.]+\s+$Name\s*$' | Set-Content `$h"
   630â†’        $netshCmd = "netsh interface portproxy delete v4tov4 listenport=$sshPort listenaddress=0.0.0.0 2>`$null; " +
   631â†’                    $hostsCmd
   632â†’        Start-Process powershell -Verb RunAs -ArgumentList "-Command", $netshCmd -Wait -WindowStyle Hidden
   633â†’    } catch {
   634â†’        Write-Host "  WARNING: Could not remove port forwarding/hosts entry (requires admin)."
   635â†’    }
   636â†’    Write-Host "Instance '$Name' destroyed."
   637â†’}
   638â†’
   639â†’# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   640â†’# Dispatch
   641â†’# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   642â†’
   643â†’switch ($Action) {
   644â†’    "create"  { Invoke-Create }
   645â†’    "destroy" { Invoke-Destroy }
   646â†’    "list"    { wsl --list --verbose }
   647â†’}
   648â†’

<#
.SYNOPSIS
    Create, destroy, or list WSL2 development VMs.

.DESCRIPTION
    One-file bootstrap for provisioned WSL2 dev environments.
    Downloads Ubuntu rootfs, creates a WSL2 instance, and runs
    Ansible to install all development tooling.

    All configuration lives in a single JSON file at
    %LOCALAPPDATA%\WSLDev\config.json. First run prompts to
    create it. Per-instance settings (SSH port) are in the
    Instances map.

    Resilient to failures: if provisioning fails partway (bad
    passphrase, network error, etc.), re-running the script
    detects the existing instance and re-provisions it.

.EXAMPLE
    .\wsl-dev.ps1                              # Prompts for hostname, creates instance
    .\wsl-dev.ps1 -Name myhost                 # Creates instance named "myhost"
    .\wsl-dev.ps1 -Action destroy -Name myhost # Destroys named instance
    .\wsl-dev.ps1 -Action list                 # Lists all WSL instances
#>

param(
    [ValidateSet("create", "destroy", "list")]
    [string]$Action = "create",

    [string]$Name
)

$ErrorActionPreference = "Stop"
$instanceRoot = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\WSLDev"
$cacheDir     = "$instanceRoot\.cache"
$configPath   = "$instanceRoot\config.json"
$baseName     = "Ubuntu-24.04"  # Store distro used to create base tarball

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Config â€” single file, written incrementally as each field is collected
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

function Save-Config {
    param([hashtable]$Config)
    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path (Split-Path $configPath) -Force | Out-Null
    $Config | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5 | Set-Content $configPath
}

function Prompt-Field {
    param(
        [hashtable]$Config,
        [string]$Key,
        [string]$Prompt,
        [string]$Saved
    )
    if (-not [string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($Saved)) {
        Write-Host "  $($Key): $Saved (saved)"
        return $Saved
    }
    $value = Read-Host $Prompt
    $Config[$Key] = $value
    Save-Config $Config
    return $value
}

function Get-DevConfig {
    # Load existing config or start with an empty one
    $cfg = [ordered]@{}
    $resumed = $false
    if (Test-Path $configPath) {
        $loaded = Get-Content $configPath -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json
        foreach ($prop in $loaded.PSObject.Properties) {
            $cfg[$prop.Name] = $prop.Value
        }
        # Check if config is complete
        $required = @("User", "GitName", "GitEmail", "IdentityKeyPath", "SetupRepo", "Repos", "Instances")
        $missing = $required | Where-Object { -not $cfg.Contains($_) -or [string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($cfg[$_]) }
        # Also treat IdentityKeyPath as missing if the files don't exist on disk
        $idkPath = $cfg["IdentityKeyPath"]
        if (-not [string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($idkPath) -and (-not (Test-Path $idkPath) -or -not (Test-Path "$idkPath.pub"))) {
            Write-Host "  Saved GitHub key not found: $idkPath"
            $cfg.Remove("IdentityKeyPath")
            Save-Config $cfg
            $missing = @("IdentityKeyPath")
        }
        if (-not $missing) {
            Write-Host "Using config from $configPath"
            Write-Host "  To start fresh, delete the file and re-run.`n"
            return [PSCustomObject]$cfg
        }
        $resumed = $true
        Write-Host "Resuming setup from $configPath (some fields already saved)."
        Write-Host "  To start fresh, delete the file and re-run.`n"
    } else {
        Write-Host "First-time setup -- each answer is saved immediately.`n"
    }

    # Collect fields, skipping any that are already set
    # Default Linux username to current Windows session username (lowercase)
    if (-not $cfg.Contains("User") -or [string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($cfg["User"])) {
        $cfg["User"] = $env:USERNAME.ToLower()
        Save-Config $cfg
    }
    $cfg["User"] = Prompt-Field $cfg "User" "Linux username" $cfg["User"]

    $cfg["GitName"] = Prompt-Field $cfg "GitName" "Git author name" $cfg["GitName"]

    $cfg["GitEmail"] = Prompt-Field $cfg "GitEmail" "Git author email (for commits)" $cfg["GitEmail"]

    $savedKey = $cfg["IdentityKeyPath"]
    $savedKeyValid = (-not [string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($savedKey)) -and (Test-Path $savedKey) -and (Test-Path "$savedKey.pub")
    if ($savedKeyValid) {
        Write-Host "  IdentityKeyPath: $savedKey (saved)"
        $idKey = $savedKey
    } else {
        if (-not [string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($savedKey)) {
            Write-Host "  Saved key not found: $savedKey -- re-selecting..."
        }
        # Discover SSH private keys (files with a matching .pub, excluding VM access keys)
        $sshDir = "$env:USERPROFILE\.ssh"
        $candidates = @()
        if (Test-Path $sshDir) {
            $candidates = @(Get-ChildItem "$sshDir\*.pub" -File |
                Where-Object { $_.Name -notmatch '_wsl_' } |
                ForEach-Object { $_.FullName -replace '\.pub$', '' } |
                Where-Object { Test-Path $_ })
        }
        Write-Host ""
        Write-Host "GitHub SSH key (used for git/GitHub auth, NOT for VM access):"
        if ($candidates.Count -gt 0) {
            for ($i = 0; $i -lt $candidates.Count; $i++) {
                Write-Host "  [$($i + 1)] $($candidates[$i])"
            }
            Write-Host "  [M] Enter path manually"
            $choice = Read-Host "Select a key"
            if ($choice -match '^\d+$' -and [int]$choice -ge 1 -and [int]$choice -le $candidates.Count) {
                $idKey = $candidates[[int]$choice - 1]
            } elseif ($choice -match '^[Mm]$') {
                $idKey = Read-Host "Path to SSH private key"
            } else {
                Write-Error "Invalid selection."
                exit 1
            }
        } else {
            Write-Host "  No SSH keys found in $sshDir"
            $idKey = Read-Host "Path to SSH private key"
        }
        if (-not (Test-Path $idKey)) {
            Write-Error "Key not found at $idKey"
            exit 1
        }
        if (-not (Test-Path "$idKey.pub")) {
            Write-Error "Public key not found at $idKey.pub"
            exit 1
        }
        $cfg["IdentityKeyPath"] = $idKey
        Save-Config $cfg
    }

    $cfg["SetupRepo"] = Prompt-Field $cfg "SetupRepo" "dev-setup repo SSH URL (e.g., git@github.com:org/dev-setup.git)" $cfg["SetupRepo"]

    if (-not $cfg.Contains("Dotfiles")) {
        $dotfiles = Read-Host "Dotfiles repo SSH URL (empty to skip)"
        $cfg["Dotfiles"] = $dotfiles
        Save-Config $cfg
    } else {
        $df = $cfg["Dotfiles"]
        if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($df)) {
            Write-Host "  Dotfiles: (none)"
        } else {
            Write-Host "  Dotfiles: $df (saved)"
        }
    }

    if (-not $cfg.Contains("Repos") -or $cfg["Repos"] -eq $null) {
        $repos = @()
        Write-Host "`nProject repos to clone (SSH URLs, e.g., git@github.com:org/repo.git; empty line when done):"
        while ($true) {
            $repo = Read-Host "  repo"
            if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($repo)) { break }
            $repos += $repo
        }
        $cfg["Repos"] = $repos
        Save-Config $cfg
    } else {
        $repoList = ($cfg["Repos"] | ForEach-Object { $_ }) -join ", "
        Write-Host "  Repos: $repoList (saved)"
    }

    if (-not $cfg.Contains("Instances") -or $cfg["Instances"] -eq $null) {
        $cfg["Instances"] = [ordered]@{}
        Save-Config $cfg
    }

    Write-Host "`nConfig complete.`n"
    return [PSCustomObject]$cfg
}

function Get-InstanceConfig {
    param([PSCustomObject]$Config, [string]$InstanceName)

    # Look up per-instance settings; fall back to defaults
    $inst = $Config.Instances.PSObject.Properties | Where-Object { $_.Name -eq $InstanceName }
    if ($inst) {
        return $inst.Value
    }
    # Default for unlisted instances: auto-assign port based on instance count
    $basePort = 2222
    $existing = @($Config.Instances.PSObject.Properties).Count
    return [PSCustomObject]@{ SSHPort = $basePort + $existing }
}

function Write-Utf8NoBom {
    param([string]$Path, [string]$Content)
    # Ensure Unix line endings (LF only) â€” bash chokes on \r\n
    $Content = $Content -replace "`r`n", "`n"
    [IO.File]::WriteAllText($Path, $Content, [Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false))
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Check if a WSL2 instance exists (registered)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

function Test-WslInstance {
    param([string]$InstanceName)
    # wsl --list output contains null characters; strip them before matching
    $raw = (wsl --list --quiet 2>$null) -join "`n"
    $clean = $raw -replace "`0", ""
    return ($clean -split "`n" | ForEach-Object { $_.Trim() } | Where-Object { $_ -eq $InstanceName }).Count -gt 0
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Create
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

function Invoke-Create {
    $config = Get-DevConfig
    $user = $config.User
    $setupRepo = $config.SetupRepo

    # Use -Name as the WSL instance name and hostname
    if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($Name)) {
        # Reuse the existing instance name if there's exactly one
        $instances = @($config.Instances.PSObject.Properties)
        if ($instances.Count -eq 1) {
            $Name = $instances[0].Name
            Write-Host "  Using instance: $Name (from config)"
        } else {
            $Name = Read-Host "Hostname for this instance (used as WSL name and Linux hostname)"
            if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($Name)) {
                Write-Error "Hostname is required."
                exit 1
            }
        }
    }

    $inst = Get-InstanceConfig -Config $config -InstanceName $Name
    $sshPort = $inst.SSHPort

    # Save instance config if new
    $existingInst = $config.Instances.PSObject.Properties | Where-Object { $_.Name -eq $Name }
    if (-not $existingInst) {
        $config.Instances | Add-Member -NotePropertyName $Name -NotePropertyValue ([PSCustomObject]@{ SSHPort = $sshPort }) -Force
        $cfg = [ordered]@{}
        foreach ($prop in $config.PSObject.Properties) { $cfg[$prop.Name] = $prop.Value }
        Save-Config $cfg
    }

    $hostname = $Name

    # --- Verify Windows SSH agent has the GitHub key loaded ---
    $agentKeys = ssh-add -L 2>&1
    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0 -or $agentKeys -match "Could not open|error") {
        Write-Error "Windows SSH agent is not running. Start it with: Get-Service ssh-agent | Set-Service -StartupType Automatic; Start-Service ssh-agent"
        exit 1
    }
    if ($agentKeys -match "no identities") {
        Write-Error "No keys in Windows SSH agent. Add your GitHub key with: ssh-add $($config.IdentityKeyPath)"
        exit 1
    }
    Write-Host "  Windows SSH agent has keys loaded."

    # --- Generate VM access key (separate from identity key) ---
    $vmKeyPath = "$env:USERPROFILE\.ssh\id_ed25519_wsl_$Name"
    if (-not (Test-Path $vmKeyPath)) {
        Write-Host "Generating VM access key at $vmKeyPath ..."
        ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f $vmKeyPath -N '""' -C "wsl-$Name-access"
        if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { throw "ssh-keygen failed" }
    } else {
        Write-Host "  VM access key exists: $vmKeyPath"
    }

    # --- Step 1: Ensure WSL2 instance exists ---
    if (Test-WslInstance $Name) {
        Write-Host "Instance '$Name' already exists. Re-provisioning..."
    } else {
        # Get base tarball from the official Store image (cached after first run)
        New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $cacheDir -Force | Out-Null
        $tarball = "$cacheDir\ubuntu-noble.tar.gz"
        if (-not (Test-Path $tarball)) {
            Write-Host "Creating base image from official Ubuntu Store distro..."

            $installed = wsl --list --quiet 2>$null | Where-Object { $_ -match "^$baseName$" }
            if (-not $installed) {
                Write-Host "  Installing $baseName from Microsoft Store..."
                wsl --install $baseName --no-launch
                if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { throw "wsl --install $baseName failed" }
            }

            Write-Host "  Exporting to tarball (this takes a minute)..."
            wsl --export $baseName $tarball
            if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { throw "wsl --export failed" }

            Write-Host "  Removing temporary Store distro..."
            wsl --unregister $baseName
            Write-Host "  Base image cached at $tarball"
        } else {
            Write-Host "Using cached base image."
        }

        $instancePath = "$instanceRoot\$Name"
        Write-Host "`nCreating WSL2 instance '$Name'..."
        New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $instancePath -Force | Out-Null
        wsl --import $Name $instancePath $tarball --version 2
        if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { throw "wsl --import failed" }

        # Enable systemd
        Write-Host "Enabling systemd..."
        wsl -d $Name --exec bash -c "printf '[boot]\nsystemd=true\n' > /etc/wsl.conf"

        # Restart to activate systemd
        Write-Host "Restarting instance for systemd..."
        wsl --terminate $Name
        Start-Sleep -Seconds 3
    }

    # Ensure user exists (idempotent â€” runs on both new and re-provision)
    Write-Host "Ensuring user '$user' exists..."
    wsl -d $Name -u root --exec bash -c "set -euo pipefail; id $user &>/dev/null || useradd -m -s /bin/bash $user; usermod -aG sudo $user; echo '$user ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL' > /etc/sudoers.d/$user; chmod 440 /etc/sudoers.d/$user; grep -q '^\[user\]' /etc/wsl.conf || printf '\n[user]\ndefault=$user\n' >> /etc/wsl.conf"

    # --- Step 2: Stage files ---
    $stageDir = "$instanceRoot\.stage"
    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $stageDir -Force | Out-Null

    Copy-Item $vmKeyPath "$stageDir\vm_access_key"
    Copy-Item "$vmKeyPath.pub" "$stageDir\vm_access_key.pub"
    Copy-Item "$($config.IdentityKeyPath).pub" "$stageDir\id_ed25519.pub"

    $dotfilesRepo = if ($config.Dotfiles) { $config.Dotfiles } else { "" }

    # Get IANA timezone from the WSL instance (inherits from Windows)
    $ianaTz = (wsl -d $Name -u root --exec cat /etc/timezone 2>$null)
    if (-not $ianaTz) { $ianaTz = "Etc/UTC" }

    $extraVars = [ordered]@{
        user      = $user
        git_name  = $config.GitName
        git_email = $config.GitEmail
        ssh_port  = $sshPort
        hostname  = $hostname
        timezone  = $ianaTz
        dotfiles  = $dotfilesRepo
        repos     = @($config.Repos | ForEach-Object { $_ })
    }
    Write-Utf8NoBom "$stageDir\extra-vars.json" ($extraVars | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5)

    # Two provision scripts:
    #   1. provision-root.sh â€” runs as root: installs SSH keys, apt packages, Ansible
    #   2. provision-user.sh â€” runs as the user: git clones, Ansible (uses Windows SSH agent via ssh.exe)

    $rootScript = @"
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

USER_NAME="$user"
HOME_DIR="/home/`$USER_NAME"

# --- SSH keys ---
echo '=== Installing SSH keys ==='
mkdir -p `$HOME_DIR/.ssh
chmod 700 `$HOME_DIR/.ssh

# VM access key (from Windows) â†’ authorized_keys
cp /tmp/stage/vm_access_key.pub `$HOME_DIR/.ssh/authorized_keys

# Identity public key only (for GitHub reference; private key stays on Windows)
cp /tmp/stage/id_ed25519.pub `$HOME_DIR/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub

chown -R `$USER_NAME:`$USER_NAME `$HOME_DIR/.ssh
chmod 644 `$HOME_DIR/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub `$HOME_DIR/.ssh/authorized_keys

ssh-keyscan github.com >> `$HOME_DIR/.ssh/known_hosts 2>/dev/null
chown `$USER_NAME:`$USER_NAME `$HOME_DIR/.ssh/known_hosts

# --- System packages ---
echo '=== Installing git and Ansible ==='
apt-get update -qq
apt-get install -y -qq git software-properties-common > /dev/null
add-apt-repository --yes --update ppa:ansible/ansible > /dev/null 2>&1
apt-get install -y -qq ansible > /dev/null

echo '=== Root setup complete ==='
"@
    Write-Utf8NoBom "$stageDir\provision-root.sh" $rootScript

    $userScript = @"
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

SETUP_REPO="$setupRepo"

# --- Use Windows SSH agent via ssh.exe (no private key copy needed) ---
echo '=== Configuring SSH for provisioning ==='
WIN_SSH="/mnt/c/Windows/System32/OpenSSH/ssh.exe"
if [ ! -x "`$WIN_SSH" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Windows SSH not found at `$WIN_SSH"
    exit 1
fi
export GIT_SSH_COMMAND="`$WIN_SSH"
echo "  Using Windows SSH agent via `$WIN_SSH"

# --- Clone dev-setup repo ---
echo ''
echo '=== Cloning dev-setup repo ==='
if [ -d ~/dev-setup ]; then
    echo '  Repo exists, pulling latest...'
    cd ~/dev-setup && git pull --ff-only
else
    echo "  Cloning `$SETUP_REPO ..."
    git clone --depth 1 "`$SETUP_REPO" ~/dev-setup
fi

# --- Run Ansible (installs all packages) ---
echo ''
echo '=== Running Ansible playbook ==='
cd ~/dev-setup
sudo --preserve-env=GIT_SSH_COMMAND ansible-playbook playbook.yml --diff -e @/tmp/stage/extra-vars.json

# --- Dotfiles (after packages so tools like stow are available) ---
DOTFILES_REPO="$dotfilesRepo"
if [ -n "`$DOTFILES_REPO" ]; then
    echo ''
    echo '=== Installing dotfiles ==='
    if [ -d ~/dotfiles ]; then
        echo '  Dotfiles repo exists, pulling latest...'
        cd ~/dotfiles && git pull --ff-only
    else
        echo "  Cloning `$DOTFILES_REPO ..."
        git clone "`$DOTFILES_REPO" ~/dotfiles
    fi
    if [ -x ~/dotfiles/install.sh ]; then
        echo '  Running install.sh...'
        cd ~/dotfiles && ./install.sh
    fi
fi

# --- Cleanup ---
sudo rm -rf /tmp/stage

echo ''
echo '========================================='
echo '  Provisioning complete.'
echo '========================================='
echo ''
echo 'Remaining manual steps:'
echo '  1. netbird up         (authenticate to mesh VPN)'
echo ''
"@
    Write-Utf8NoBom "$stageDir\provision-user.sh" $userScript

    # --- Step 3: Copy staging files in and run provisioning ---
    Write-Host "`nProvisioning (this takes a few minutes)..."

    $wslStageDir = wsl -d $Name --exec wslpath -a "$stageDir"

    # Copy stage files in (as root)
    wsl -d $Name -u root --exec bash -c "cp -r $wslStageDir /tmp/stage && chmod +x /tmp/stage/*.sh"

    # Root phase: SSH keys + system packages (explicitly as root)
    Write-Host ""
    wsl -d $Name -u root --exec /tmp/stage/provision-root.sh
    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) {
        Write-Host "`nRoot provisioning failed. Re-run this script to retry."
        Remove-Item -Recurse -Force $stageDir -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
        exit 1
    }

    # User phase: ssh-agent, git clones, Ansible (explicitly as the user)
    Write-Host ""
    wsl -d $Name -u $user --exec /tmp/stage/provision-user.sh
    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) {
        Write-Host "`nUser provisioning failed. Re-run this script to retry."
        Remove-Item -Recurse -Force $stageDir -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
        exit 1
    }

    # Clean up staging dir on Windows side
    Remove-Item -Recurse -Force $stageDir -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

    # --- Step 4: Port forwarding + hostname alias (non-fatal, elevates for netsh/hosts) ---
    Write-Host "`nSetting up SSH port forwarding (port $sshPort) and hostname alias..."
    try {
        $hostsCmd = "`$h = '$env:SystemRoot\System32\drivers\etc\hosts'; " +
                    "`$entry = '127.0.0.1 $hostname'; " +
                    "(Get-Content `$h) -notmatch '^\s*[\d\.]+\s+$hostname\s*$' | Set-Content `$h; " +
                    "Add-Content `$h `$entry"
        $netshCmd = "netsh interface portproxy delete v4tov4 listenport=$sshPort listenaddress=0.0.0.0 2>`$null; " +
                    "netsh interface portproxy add v4tov4 listenport=$sshPort listenaddress=0.0.0.0 connectport=$sshPort connectaddress=localhost; " +
                    $hostsCmd
        Start-Process powershell -Verb RunAs -ArgumentList "-Command", $netshCmd -Wait -WindowStyle Hidden
        # Verify the rule was actually created
        $proxy = netsh interface portproxy show v4tov4 2>$null
        if ($proxy -match "$sshPort") {
            Write-Host "  Port forwarding configured."
        } else {
            Write-Host "  WARNING: Port forwarding rule not found. The elevated command may have failed."
            Write-Host "  You can still access the instance via: wsl -d $Name"
        }
    } catch [System.InvalidOperationException] {
        Write-Host "  WARNING: UAC prompt was declined. Port forwarding not configured."
        Write-Host "  You can still access the instance via: wsl -d $Name"
    } catch {
        Write-Host "  WARNING: Port forwarding failed: $_"
        Write-Host "  You can still access the instance via: wsl -d $Name"
    }

    # --- Step 5: Auto-start WSL instance at logon ---
    $taskName = "WSL-AutoStart-$Name"
    $existingTask = Get-ScheduledTask -TaskName $taskName -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    if ($existingTask) {
        Write-Host "  Scheduled task '$taskName' already exists."
    } else {
        Write-Host "Registering scheduled task to auto-start '$Name' at logon..."
        try {
            $action = New-ScheduledTaskAction -Execute "wsl.exe" -Argument "-d $Name -- sleep infinity"
            $trigger = New-ScheduledTaskTrigger -AtLogOn
            $trigger.UserId = [System.Security.Principal.WindowsIdentity]::GetCurrent().Name
            $settings = New-ScheduledTaskSettingsSet -AllowStartIfOnBatteries -DontStopIfGoingOnBatteries -ExecutionTimeLimit ([TimeSpan]::Zero)
            Register-ScheduledTask -TaskName $taskName -Action $action -Trigger $trigger -Settings $settings | Out-Null
            Write-Host "  Scheduled task '$taskName' registered."
        } catch {
            Write-Host "  WARNING: Could not register scheduled task: $_"
            Write-Host "  WSL instance won't auto-start. You can start it manually with: wsl -d $Name"
        }
    }

    # --- Step 6: Windows SSH config + agent ---
    $sshConfigPath = "$env:USERPROFILE\.ssh\config"
    $hostBlock = @"

Host $hostname
    HostName 127.0.0.1
    Port $sshPort
    User $user
    IdentityFile $vmKeyPath
"@
    # Remove any existing block for this host, then append
    if (Test-Path $sshConfigPath) {
        $content = Get-Content $sshConfigPath -Raw
        $content = $content -replace "(?m)\r?\nHost $hostname\r?\n(\s+\S[^\n]*\r?\n)*", ""
        Set-Content $sshConfigPath $content.TrimEnd()
    }
    Add-Content $sshConfigPath $hostBlock
    Write-Host "  SSH config updated: Host $hostname -> localhost:$sshPort"

    # Add VM access key to Windows SSH agent
    ssh-add $vmKeyPath 2>$null
    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) {
        Write-Host "  VM access key added to Windows SSH agent."
    } else {
        Write-Host "  WARNING: Could not add VM key to SSH agent. Run: ssh-add $vmKeyPath"
    }

    Write-Host ""
    Write-Host "Instance '$Name' is ready."
    Write-Host "  Enter:  wsl -d $Name"
    Write-Host "  SSH:    ssh $hostname"
    Write-Host ""
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Destroy
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

function Invoke-Destroy {
    if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($Name)) {
        Write-Error "Destroy requires -Name <instance>."
        exit 1
    }
    $config = Get-DevConfig
    $inst = Get-InstanceConfig -Config $config -InstanceName $Name
    $sshPort = $inst.SSHPort

    Write-Host "Destroying instance '$Name'..."
    # Remove auto-start scheduled task
    $taskName = "WSL-AutoStart-$Name"
    $existingTask = Get-ScheduledTask -TaskName $taskName -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    if ($existingTask) {
        Unregister-ScheduledTask -TaskName $taskName -Confirm:$false
        Write-Host "  Removed scheduled task '$taskName'"
    }
    wsl --unregister $Name 2>$null
    $instancePath = "$instanceRoot\$Name"
    if (Test-Path $instancePath) {
        Remove-Item -Recurse -Force $instancePath
    }
    # Remove VM access key pair
    $vmKeyPath = "$env:USERPROFILE\.ssh\id_ed25519_wsl_$Name"
    if (Test-Path $vmKeyPath) {
        Remove-Item -Force $vmKeyPath
        Write-Host "  Removed $vmKeyPath"
    }
    if (Test-Path "$vmKeyPath.pub") {
        Remove-Item -Force "$vmKeyPath.pub"
        Write-Host "  Removed $vmKeyPath.pub"
    }
    # Remove SSH config block
    $sshConfigPath = "$env:USERPROFILE\.ssh\config"
    if (Test-Path $sshConfigPath) {
        $content = Get-Content $sshConfigPath -Raw
        $content = $content -replace "(?m)\r?\nHost $Name\r?\n(\s+\S[^\n]*\r?\n)*", ""
        Set-Content $sshConfigPath $content.TrimEnd()
        Write-Host "  Removed SSH config for Host $Name"
    }
    # Remove key from Windows SSH agent
    ssh-add -d $vmKeyPath 2>$null
    try {
        $hostsCmd = "`$h = '$env:SystemRoot\System32\drivers\etc\hosts'; " +
                    "(Get-Content `$h) -notmatch '^\s*[\d\.]+\s+$Name\s*$' | Set-Content `$h"
        $netshCmd = "netsh interface portproxy delete v4tov4 listenport=$sshPort listenaddress=0.0.0.0 2>`$null; " +
                    $hostsCmd
        Start-Process powershell -Verb RunAs -ArgumentList "-Command", $netshCmd -Wait -WindowStyle Hidden
    } catch {
        Write-Host "  WARNING: Could not remove port forwarding/hosts entry (requires admin)."
    }
    Write-Host "Instance '$Name' destroyed."
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Dispatch
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

switch ($Action) {
    "create"  { Invoke-Create }
    "destroy" { Invoke-Destroy }
    "list"    { wsl --list --verbose }
}

- name: Ensure .bash_profile sources .bashrc then WSL agent override
  copy:
    dest: "/home/{{ user }}/.bash_profile"
    content: |
      # Source .bashrc for login shells (e.g. SSH sessions)
      if [ -f "$HOME/.bashrc" ]; then
          . "$HOME/.bashrc"
      fi
      # Override SSH_AUTH_SOCK after .bashrc (so it wins over keychain etc.)
      if [ -f /etc/profile.d/wsl-ssh-agent.sh ]; then
          . /etc/profile.d/wsl-ssh-agent.sh
      fi
    owner: "{{ user }}"
    group: "{{ user }}"
    mode: "0644"
    force: false

- name: Configure git settings
  shell: |
    su -l {{ user }} -c 'git config --global {{ item.key }} "{{ item.value }}"'
  loop:
    - { key: user.name, value: "{{ git_name }}" }
    - { key: user.email, value: "{{ git_email }}" }
    - { key: init.defaultBranch, value: main }
    - { key: pull.rebase, value: "true" }
  changed_when: false

- name: Create project directory
  file:
    path: "/home/{{ user }}/project"
    state: directory
    owner: "{{ user }}"
    group: "{{ user }}"

- name: Clone project repos
  shell: |
    SSH_AUTH_SOCK="{{ lookup('env', 'SSH_AUTH_SOCK') }}"
    GIT_SSH_COMMAND="{{ lookup('env', 'GIT_SSH_COMMAND') }}"
    SSH_ENV=""
    if [ -n "$GIT_SSH_COMMAND" ]; then
      SSH_ENV="GIT_SSH_COMMAND=$GIT_SSH_COMMAND"
    elif [ -n "$SSH_AUTH_SOCK" ]; then
      SSH_ENV="SSH_AUTH_SOCK=$SSH_AUTH_SOCK"
    fi
    dest="/home/{{ user }}/project/{{ item | regex_replace('.*[:/]([^/]+?)(\\.git)?$', '\\1') }}"
    if [ -d "$dest" ]; then
      su -l {{ user }} -c "cd $dest && $SSH_ENV git pull --ff-only"
    else
      su -l {{ user }} -c "$SSH_ENV git clone '{{ item }}' '$dest'"
    fi
  loop: "{{ repos }}"
  when: repos | length > 0

content.text:
Good, now I understand the full picture. The `repos` list is statically configured in `config.json` and passed through to Ansible. You want to dynamically discover repos you've committed to recently via the GitHub API.

Yes â€” the GitHub API has exactly what you need. The [Events API](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/activity/events) exposes `PushEvent` entries for your user, and the [Search API](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/search) can find commits by author. The cleanest approach is using `gh` CLI which handles auth automatically.

Here's what I'd recommend:

**Option A: Add a step in `provision-user.sh` (inside WSL)** â€” but `gh` isn't installed yet at that point and auth would be tricky.

**Option B: Add it to `wsl-dev.ps1` on the Windows side** â€” run `gh` before provisioning to dynamically populate the repos list. This is the natural fit since config is assembled on the Windows side and `gh` is likely already authenticated there.

The approach would be:

1. Use `gh api` on Windows to query your recent push activity
2. Merge those repos with any manually-configured repos
3. Pass the combined list to Ansible as usual

Here's the specific API call that works well:

```powershell
# Get repos you've pushed to in the last 60 days
gh api "/search/commits?q=author:USERNAME+committer-date:>2026-01-05&sort=committer-date&per_page=100" \
  --jq '[.items[].repository.ssh_url] | unique'
```

The search commits API is better than the events API here because events only go back 90 days but are paginated at 30/page with a 300-event cap, while commit search directly filters by date and gives you the SSH clone URLs.

Want me to implement this as a function in `wsl-dev.ps1` that auto-discovers recent repos and merges them with the manually configured ones? I'd add it as an opt-in step during `Invoke-Create`, something like:

```
Discover repos you've committed to in the last 60 days? [Y/n]
```

Or would you prefer it to always run automatically without prompting?

content.text:
The GitHub API requires a personal access token (PAT) or OAuth token for authenticated requests â€” SSH keys don't work for API auth. However, `gh auth login` supports authenticating via SSH key for **git operations**, but API calls still need a token under the hood.

That said, `gh` has a nice interactive login flow that works well here:

- `gh auth login` can do a **device code flow** â€” it shows a code, you open a browser URL on any machine, paste the code, and you're authenticated. No token to manually create or copy.
- Once authenticated, `gh` stores the token and can query the API.

So the practical flow would be:

1. Install `gh` on the VM (add it as a role or to an existing one)
2. After provisioning, run `gh auth login --web` interactively (one-time manual step)
3. Then a script/command can query recent repos and clone them

- name: Check if NetBird is installed
  command: which netbird
  register: netbird_check
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: false

- name: Install NetBird
  shell: curl -fsSL https://pkgs.netbird.io/install.sh | sh
  when: netbird_check.rc != 0

- name: Enable NetBird service
  systemd:
    name: netbird
    enabled: true
    state: started

# dev-setup

Ansible-provisioned WSL2 development VMs. One PowerShell command creates a
fully configured Ubuntu instance with Go, Rust, Node.js, Python, Docker,
PostgreSQL client, Playwright GUI support, SSH, and NetBird mesh VPN.

## Prerequisites

- Windows 11 (or Windows 10 21H2+) with WSL2 enabled
- An SSH keypair (`~/.ssh/id_ed25519` + `.pub`) added to your GitHub account

To enable WSL2 if not already:

```powershell
wsl --install --no-distribution
```

## Quick start

```powershell
# Download and run (no git required on the host)
Invoke-WebRequest https://raw.githubusercontent.com/YOU/dev-setup/main/wsl-dev.ps1 -OutFile $env:TEMP\wsl-dev.ps1; & $env:TEMP\wsl-dev.ps1
```

First run prompts for:
- Linux username
- Git author name and email
- Path to your SSH private key
- SSH URL of this dev-setup repo
- Dotfiles repo SSH URL (optional â€” cloned to `~/dotfiles`, runs `install.sh`)
- Project repos to clone (SSH URLs)

Everything is saved to a single config file at
`%LOCALAPPDATA%\WSLDev\config.json` and reused on rebuilds.

### Config file example

```json
{
  "User": "jdoe",
  "GitName": "Jane Doe",
  "GitEmail": "jane@example.com",
  "SSHKeyPath": "C:\\Users\\jdoe\\.ssh\\id_ed25519",
  "SetupRepo": "git@github.com:jdoe/dev-setup.git",
  "Dotfiles": "git@github.com:jdoe/dotfiles.git",
  "Repos": [
    "git@github.com:jdoe/project-alpha.git",
    "git@github.com:jdoe/project-beta.git"
  ],
  "Instances": {
    "dev":   { "SSHPort": 2222 },
    "dev-2": { "SSHPort": 2223 }
  }
}
```

Edit this file directly to change repos, add instances, or update identity.
Delete it to re-prompt on next create.

## Usage

```powershell
.\wsl-dev.ps1                              # Create default "dev" instance
.\wsl-dev.ps1 -Name dev-2                  # Create a second instance
.\wsl-dev.ps1 -Action destroy              # Destroy default instance
.\wsl-dev.ps1 -Action destroy -Name dev-2  # Destroy named instance
.\wsl-dev.ps1 -Action list                 # List all WSL instances
```

The `-Name` parameter selects which entry in the `Instances` map to use for
SSH port assignment. Unlisted names get an auto-assigned port.

## What gets installed

| Role | What it provides |
|---|---|
| base | build-essential, git, curl, tmux, ripgrep, glow, jq, cmake, locales |
| docker | Docker Engine + docker-compose-plugin (not Docker Desktop) |
| golang | Go (version in `vars/main.yml`), air, golangci-lint |
| rust | Rust stable via rustup, libz3-dev, libclang-dev |
| nodejs | Node.js + pnpm |
| python | Python 3 + pip + pyyaml |
| postgresql | PostgreSQL client + libpq-dev (server runs via Docker) |
| playwright | Browser GUI dependencies for headed Playwright under WSLg |
| ssh | OpenSSH server on configurable port |
| netbird | NetBird mesh VPN agent |
| project-tools | Claude CLI, beads issue tracker |
| user | Git config, project directory, repo cloning via SSH |

## Nuke and rebuild

```powershell
.\wsl-dev.ps1 -Action destroy
.\wsl-dev.ps1 -Action create
# Re-clones repos, re-provisions everything. ~5-10 minutes.
```

Config is preserved across rebuilds. The Ubuntu rootfs tarball is cached at
`%LOCALAPPDATA%\WSLDev\.cache\` after first download.

## Remote access

After provisioning, run `netbird up` inside the VM to authenticate. Then
from any machine on your NetBird network:

```bash
ssh -p 2222 youruser@vm-hostname
```

## Multiple instances

Per-instance SSH ports are defined in the `Instances` map in `config.json`.
Each instance gets its own filesystem, SSH port, and NetBird identity:

```powershell
.\wsl-dev.ps1 -Name dev                   # uses SSHPort 2222
.\wsl-dev.ps1 -Name dev-2                 # uses SSHPort 2223
```

## Re-provisioning (without rebuilding)

To update an existing instance after changing the playbook:

```bash
# Inside the WSL2 instance
cd ~/dev-setup
git pull
ansible-playbook playbook.yml --diff
```

## Customizing

- **Versions**: edit `vars/main.yml`
- **Add a tool**: create a new role under `roles/`, add it to `playbook.yml`
- **Change repos or identity**: edit `%LOCALAPPDATA%\WSLDev\config.json`
- **Add an instance**: add an entry to the `Instances` map in `config.json`

## File layout

```
wsl-dev.ps1           PowerShell entry point (runs on Windows)
ansible.cfg           Ansible configuration
inventory.yml         Localhost inventory
playbook.yml          Main playbook
vars/main.yml         Version pins and defaults
roles/
  base/               System packages, locale, timezone
  docker/             Docker Engine (not Desktop)
  golang/             Go + dev tools
  rust/               Rust toolchain + system libs
  nodejs/             Node.js + pnpm
  python/             Python 3 + pip + pyyaml
  postgresql/         PostgreSQL client libraries
  playwright/         Browser deps for headed mode under WSLg
  ssh/                OpenSSH server
  netbird/            NetBird mesh VPN
  project-tools/      Claude CLI, beads
  user/               Git config, repo cloning
```

# Post-provision manual setup script

## Context

After Ansible provisioning, the user must manually run `netbird up` (browser auth) and will now also want to authenticate with GitHub CLI and clone recently-active repos. These are interactive steps that can't be automated in Ansible. A single script in `~/setup/` will handle all post-provision manual steps.

## Changes

### 1. Install `gh` CLI â€” new Ansible role `roles/github-cli/`

`roles/github-cli/tasks/main.yml`:
- Add the GitHub CLI apt repository and install `gh`
- Standard pattern matching other roles (e.g., netbird)

Add `github-cli` to `playbook.yml` (before `user` role).

### 2. Create `~/setup/post-provision.sh` via the `user` role

Add a task to `roles/user/tasks/main.yml` that templates a script to `~/setup/post-provision.sh`. The script will:

1. **`gh auth login`** â€” device code flow (interactive, opens browser)
2. **Clone recent repos** â€” uses `gh api` to search commits by the user's configured git email in the last 60 days, extracts unique repo SSH URLs, skips any already cloned in `~/project/`
3. **`netbird up`** â€” interactive browser auth for mesh VPN

The git email is already available as an Ansible variable (`git_email`), so it can be templated into the script.

### 3. Update provisioning output

Update the "manual steps" message in `wsl-dev.ps1` to reference `~/setup/post-provision.sh` instead of listing `netbird up` alone.

## Files to modify

- `roles/github-cli/tasks/main.yml` â€” **new file**
- `playbook.yml` â€” add `github-cli` role
- `roles/user/tasks/main.yml` â€” add task to create `~/setup/post-provision.sh`
- `wsl-dev.ps1` â€” update post-provision instructions (line ~466)

## Verification

- Run `ansible-playbook playbook.yml --diff` on an existing instance
- Confirm `gh` is installed
- Confirm `~/setup/post-provision.sh` exists with correct content
- Run `~/setup/post-provision.sh` and verify it prompts for gh auth, clones repos, and runs netbird up
